tasks.title: Unified closeout benchmark protocol
tasks.description: Produce one canonical reproducible benchmark suite for render/input/sensor with deterministic seeds, fixed thresholds, and consolidated artifact index.
acceptance_criteria: Unified benchmark suite runs on demand with deterministic seeds and emits per-scenario pass/fail verdicts.
Frame time thresholds: p50 <= 16.7ms, p95 <= 25.0ms, p99 <= 33.3ms for required interactive scenarios.
Input-to-present thresholds: p95 <= 33.3ms and p99 <= 50.0ms for burst input scenarios.
Incremental-present ratio >= 90% across required interactive scenarios unless approved T-2804 exceptions apply.
Dropped-frame budget <= 1.0% per scenario run (<= 2.0% for resize stress only).
Resize burst recovery <= 1.0s with no crash/hang.
Determinism replay protocol: 8 fixed seeds x 10 runs per seed per scenario; required digest/invariant mismatch count = 0.

tasks.title: Vulkan readiness decision gate (in-scope vs deferred)
tasks.description: Issue a single explicit Vulkan status for objective signoff and remove ambiguity across docs/evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Single clear Vulkan status for this objective is DEFERRED_WITH_GUARDRAILS and is consistent across docs/evidence.
Vulkan certified-scenario evidence meets T-2801 frame/input/drop thresholds.
Resize burst suite shows no crash/hang and recovery to stable present loop <= 1.0s.
Automatic fallback engages on Vulkan instability while protocol semantics/AppContext behavior remain identical.
RenderTarget contract remains unchanged and no app-layer backend-internal branching is introduced.
Determinism replay evidence aligns with T-2803 replay requirements.
tasks.decision_text: For P-026 runtime performance hardening closeout, Vulkan is treated as a performance-validated but non-primary execution path. Objective signoff does not require Vulkan universal production readiness across all hardware/driver combinations. Objective signoff requires: (1) no architecture boundary violations, (2) deterministic behavior on required seeded suites, (3) stable fallback-blit parity, and (4) explicit runtime guardrails that prevent Vulkan-path instability from impacting app protocol compatibility or responsiveness guarantees.
disallowed_wording: Vulkan is fully production-ready on all devices.
Objective closeout proves backend-independent identical performance.
safety_test_checklist: Verify Vulkan deferred-with-guardrails decision text is the only normative Vulkan statement across closeout artifacts.
Assert fallback activation path preserves AppContext/protocol semantics under Vulkan instability.
Verify no app-layer branching on backend internals and RenderTarget contract remains unchanged.
Run resize-burst safety checks with crash/hang detection and <=1.0s recovery gate.

tasks.title: Snapshot immutability and copy-elimination safety validation
tasks.description: Prove copy-elimination refactors preserve ownership invariants and determinism without aliasing/mutation regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Invariant spec defines MUST/MUST NOT snapshot ownership and mutability rules.
Published revisioned snapshots are immutable after publication and consumers cannot mutate snapshot memory.
Producer publishes new revision before any write that could affect previously published state; mutable backing storage for published revisions is isolated via copy-on-write/equivalent.
Revisioned reads are bound to exactly one monotonic revision id with no torn-frame reads and read-after-publish visibility for frame + dirty rect metadata.
Repeated reads of same revision id are byte-identical and dirty rects correspond only to that revision content.
Regression tests cover immutability, torn-read concurrency, revision stability, dirty-rect consistency, and fallback parity.
Determinism replay protocol passes with 8 seeds x 10 runs and zero digest/invariant mismatches per required scenario.
safety_test_checklist: Immutability guard test: attempted snapshot mutation must fail or have no effect.
Concurrent publish/read torn-frame tests must show no mixed-revision output.
Revision stability checks: repeated reads for same revision are byte-identical.
Dirty-rect consistency checks validate rect metadata maps to the same revision content.
Determinism replay safety gate: 8 seeds x 10 runs with zero digest/invariant mismatches.

tasks.title: Incremental-present coverage closure
tasks.description: Close interactive scenario gaps where full-frame presents still occur by optimization or explicit bounded exception policy.
acceptance_criteria: Scenario matrix includes: vertical scroll (slow+fling), horizontal pan, drag interaction, mixed burst input, sensor overlay interaction, resize+interaction overlap, idle-to-burst transition, and full-screen invalidation control case.
Target incremental ratios are met: vertical >=95%, horizontal >=92%, drag >=85%, mixed burst >=88%, sensor overlay >=90%, resize overlap >=75%, idle-to-burst >=85%.
Full-screen invalidation control case is allowed full-frame baseline with no incremental ratio requirement.
Exceptions only for explicit full-surface effects, full-canvas transforms, swapchain-recreate windows, or protocol-declared full invalidation.
Exception full-frame share in required non-control scenarios <=15% and consecutive full-frame bursts outside exception windows <=8 frames.
No visual regression artifacts: golden frame digest checks, dirty-rect continuity, tearing/ghosting edge scans, z-order correctness, and clamp correctness for absolute/delta RGBA paths.
safety_test_checklist: Run artifact scan for tearing/ghosting at dirty-rect boundaries in required scenarios.
Validate golden-frame digests and frame continuity under scroll/drag/burst paths.
Verify z-order correctness and clamp correctness (absolute/delta RGBA paths).
Enforce exception policy caps: <=15% non-control full-frame share and <=8 consecutive frames outside exception windows.
Fail any scenario below target ratio without approved exception rationale.

tasks.title: Evidence reconciliation and final signoff packet
tasks.description: Publish one audit-grade closeout packet with reconciled task/board/evidence status and final go/no-go checklist.
acceptance_criteria: Single source closeout packet maps each claim to artifacts/tests/docs with no unresolved contradictions.
Go/No-Go checklist validates: boundary contracts, determinism, incremental-present policy, Vulkan decision consistency, and risk closure.

Minimum evidence bundle includes unified benchmark report, determinism replay report, snapshot invariant report, incremental-present report, Vulkan gate packet, protocol compatibility note, and consolidated reconciliation map.

Any blocker condition forces No-Go: boundary contract violation, required digest/invariant mismatch, conflicting Vulkan wording, scenario target miss without approved exception, unresolved high-severity risk, or non-reproducible evidence.

Reference temporal rule snippet: `"camera_k_hat": 0` and `"world_plane_k_hat_rule": "strictly_less_than_zero"`.
Reference plane schema snippet: `if kind=camera then k_hat_index const 0; if kind=world then k_hat_index exclusiveMaximum 0 and attachment required`.

tasks.title: Implement cross-file invariant validator for manifest/plane/route/frame references
notes: Enforce invariants: camera exists + kind camera + k_hat_index==0; world planes k_hat_index<0; no duplicate ids; no attachment cycles; route/startup/frame ref constraints.
Reference manifest required fields snippet: `required: [planes_protocol_version, camera_plane_id, planes]`.

tasks.title: Define strict vs permissive mode policy and diagnostics contract
notes: Strict mode must hard-fail invalid schema/reference data.
Permissive mode allowed only for explicit compatibility windows with warnings.

tasks.title: Define canonical IR mapping contract for split files and monolith adapter
notes: Reference IR types snippet: `PlanesAppIR(ir_version="planes-ir-v2-basis", app, frames, planes, components, routes)` and compiler entrypoint `compile_to_ir(app_toml_path)`.
Canonical runtime basis is u/v/w only; aliases x/y/z and i_hat/j_hat/k_hat must normalize pre-runtime.

tasks.title: Implement split-file compiler path to canonical Planes IR
notes: Reference IR types snippet: `PlanesAppIR(ir_version="planes-ir-v2-basis", app, frames, planes, components, routes)` and compiler entrypoint `compile_to_ir(app_toml_path)`.
Runtime must receive canonical IR only (no split-file direct runtime bypass).

tasks.title: Implement monolith compatibility adapter to canonical Planes IR
notes: Adapter path required: monolith -> canonical IR.

tasks.title: Implement parity suite for monolith vs split canonical equivalence
notes: Parity must prove ordering/transforms/rounding/hit-test precedence equivalence for equivalent inputs.

tasks.title: Integrate runtime to consume canonical IR only
notes: Remove/disable any runtime path that consumes non-canonical IR structures directly.

tasks.title: Enforce deterministic transform/render/hit-test/compositing rules in runtime
notes: Transform order fixed: `T_parent * T_attachment * T_motion * T_component`.
Render order: planes by ascending k_hat_index with camera(k=0) last; components by z_local asc, mount_order asc, component_id asc.
Hit-test order is reverse draw order; compositing absolute overwrite, delta add+clamp [0,255].

tasks.title: Normalize device-native orientation to canonical u/v/w before app transforms
